summary: Compact assistant/runtime guidance that separates system governance, editable project rules, and the assistant workflow guide.

file_roles.CONTRIBUTING.md: Authoritative human commit and branch protocol.
file_roles.AGENTS.md: Repo-wide system governance, routing, verification, and completion policy.
file_roles..verlyn/runtime_context.json: Compact runtime summary for assistants and compressed sessions.
file_roles.RULES.md: Editable project-specific guidance for people and assistants.
file_roles.Documentation/guides/VERLYN_AGENT_WORKFL: Single assistant workflow guide with startup order, installed CLI commands, session loop, and completion rules.

control_path_priority: Web UI workflow surfaces for run creation, onboarding, and settings
If the installed Verlyn product path is missing or blocked, stop and record a Verlyn workflow blocker instead of bypassing Verlyn
workflow_defaults: Treat Verlyn-managed repo binding, changes, work items, reviews, delivery state, and evidence as the active workflow source of truth.
Read RULES.md for project-owned guidance; it may be empty on fresh installs, and AGENTS.md remains authoritative for system policy.

After session compaction, compressed-summary recovery, or other resume paths, reread AGENTS.md, RULES.md, .verlyn/runtime_context.json, Documentation/AI_USAGE_POLICY.md, and Documentation/guides/VERLYN_AGENT_WORKFLOW.md before continuing.
Run verlyn auth status, verlyn workflow assistant-startup --json, and verlyn target show --json after resume so the local CLI identity, edit route, and repo binding are explicit.

The first user-facing response after compaction or resume must explicitly say that governance was reloaded and required repo rules were reread; hidden command output, receipt files, or web-only indicators are not enough.
Use Verlyn-managed change/work-item state before manual code edits; use a recorded active change or an explicit direct-work exception instead of relying on memory.
